source_jobs.title: Optical Engineer (f/m/d)
source_jobs.description:
About Focused Energy

Focused Energy is at the forefront of developing NeoLyte—a pioneering initiative in compact, laser-driven radiation sources (LDRS) utilizing high-power laser technology. Our dual-mode LDRS platform integrates MeV X-rays and fast neutrons within a single, modular system, enabling real-time, non-destructive inspection of even the most complex and shielded materials. NeoLyte’s technology is transforming critical areas such as nuclear waste analysis, contraband detection, infrastructure inspection, and medical isotope production. With its unparalleled resolution, material specificity, and deployment flexibility, our LDRS platform unlocks opportunities far beyond conventional systems.

Our multidisciplinary team brings together experts in laser and plasma physics, engineering, and AI. We are establishing our first pilot facility in Biblis, Germany, and advancing rapidly toward market readiness and commercial deployment. Join us to shape this deep-tech venture from the ground up.

As our Optical Engineer, you will be pivotal in conceptualizing and delivering advanced optical systems that are central to our product roadmap. You will lead projects from concept to production, collaborating closely with cross-functional teams to deliver state-of-the-art optical solutions.






WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Design and specify concept and detailed designs for complex optical systems, aligned with functional and performance requirements.

 - Lead the integration of optical components with mechanical, electrical, and software subsystems, ensuring seamless product functionality.

 - Take ownership of optical component development, delivering project and technical deliverables weighing quality, cost, time and risk.

 - Develop and implement qualification and validation test plans to verify optical system performance and reliability.

 - Innovate and push the boundaries of optical technology by researching and developing novel optical concepts and solutions.

 - Identify and align critical suppliers to realise optics design into functional hardware.

 - Participate actively in design reviews, ensuring manufacturability and adherence to regulatory and quality standards.

 - Collaborate within multidiscipline scientific and engineering teams to find pragmatic engineering solutions towards the project objectives.



What you’ll bring

 - Hands-on Experience: At least three years of experience designing and developing advanced optical systems, preferably including high-power laser systems.

 - Expertise in Optical Design: A very good knowledge of optical design principles, materials, coatings, and testing methods. Demonstrated experience in ultra-short pulse laser systems (fs or ps-regime) is highly desirable.

 - Simulation Skills: Substantial experience in simulating optical designs and components (preferably ZEMAX). Experience in opto-thermal mechanical design simulation is a significant advantage.

 - Precision Opto-Mechanical Design: Solid knowledge of opto-mechanical design principles to support integration efforts.

 - A good understanding of photonics components, their specifications and integration in advanced high powered laser optical systems.

 - Experience of optomechanical MAIT and interaction with optical component suppliers is highly desirable.

 - Analytical and Problem-Solving Skills: Excellent analytical and problem-solving skills with a proven track record of developing and validating innovative solutions.

 - Communication Skills: Proactive communicator with the ability to clearly present complex optical concepts to diverse stakeholders.





Ready to revolutionize inspection, safety, and advanced science? Join Focused Energy as a Optical Engineer to help define and deliver the future of laser-driven radiation source technology.

source_jobs.title: Cleanroom Technician
source_jobs.description:
Focused Energy is a fusion energy startup with the goal of generating unlimited clean energy to responsibly meet future global demand. With the use and commercialization of laser-induced inertial fusion, a clean and highly efficient power generation technology is coming within reach, suitable to ensure a secure and scalable future energy supply.

We are seeking a skilled and highly reliable Cleanroom Lab Technician to support the operation, upkeep, and continuous improvement of our cleanroom and experimental laboratory environments. You will play a critical role in maintaining contamination-controlled spaces, supporting scientists and engineers with small and mid-sized technical projects, assisting with installation and implementation work, and ensuring equipment and consumables are always available and ready. Your work will directly support high-precision laser, optical, and experimental development efforts essential to our fusion energy mission.




WHAT YOU’LL DO:

 - Cleanroom Operations & Upkeep: Execute routine and advanced cleanroom cleaning protocols, including surface wipe-downs, floor maintenance, consumable replacement, and environmental monitoring to ensure compliance with cleanliness classifications and internal standards.

 - Technical Project Support: Assist scientists and engineers with preparation, execution, and wrap-up of small and mid-sized projects—including component preparation, basic mechanical or optical setup tasks, labeling, documentation, and safe material handling.

 - Procurement & Inventory Ownership: Manage ordering, tracking, and restocking of tools, consumables, lab supplies, and specialized cleanroom equipment. Develop and maintain an organized system that ensures availability while minimizing delays or shortages.

 - Tool & Equipment Coordination: Support the installation, calibration, and basic maintenance of cleanroom equipment and laboratory tools. Coordinate with external vendors as needed for service, repairs, inspections, and deliveries.

 - Minor Construction & Installation: Perform hands-on work beyond standard cleanroom technician tasks—including assisting with infrastructure buildouts, racking installation, cable routing, light mechanical fabrication, fixture mounting, and implementation of new workflows or stations.

 - Process & Safety Compliance: Ensure strict adherence to safety procedures, contamination control protocols, PPE requirements, and material flow rules. Help identify issues proactively and support corrective actions.

 - Documentation & Continuous Improvement: Maintain accurate logs, checklists, and inventory records. Contribute to improving procedures, increasing operational efficiency, and enhancing the usability and reliability of the cleanroom environment.


WHO YOU ARE:

 - Cleanroom Experience: You understand cleanliness protocols, gowning procedures, and the importance of precision and consistency in technical environments. Experience working in cleanroom environments or other contamination-controlled labs is beneficial.

 - Hands-On Technical Skills: Comfortable with tools, fixtures, mechanical assembly, and light installation tasks. You can follow engineering instructions and support lab build-outs, equipment moves, or setup changes.

 - Organized & Detail-Driven: Strong ability to maintain order across inventory, supplies, logs, and checklists. You take pride in creating smooth, predictable, and well-structured operational systems.

 - Problem Solver & Self-Starter: You notice issues early, suggest improvements, and take initiative to resolve small problems before they become big ones. You thrive in environments where ownership and proactivity matter.

 - Procurement & Vendor Coordination: Experience managing orders, tracking shipments, comparing suppliers, and coordinating with vendors for equipment or service needs.

 - Safety & Compliance Mindset: Knowledge of lab safety protocols, including handling chemicals, equipment, and materials within cleanrooms

source_jobs.title: Reinraumtechniker
source_jobs.description:
Focused Energy ist ein Fusionsenergie-Startup mit dem Ziel, unbegrenzt saubere Energie zu erzeugen, um den zukünftigen globalen Bedarf verantwortungsvoll zu decken. Durch die Nutzung und Kommerzialisierung der laserinduzierten Trägheitsfusion rückt eine saubere und hocheffiziente Stromerzeugungstechnologie in greifbare Nähe, die eine sichere und skalierbare Energieversorgung der Zukunft gewährleisten kann.

Wir suchen einen qualifizierten und äußerst zuverlässigen Reinraumlaboranten (m/w/d) zur Unterstützung des Betriebs, der Instandhaltung und der kontinuierlichen Verbesserung unserer Reinraum- und Versuchslaborumgebungen. Sie spielen eine entscheidende Rolle bei der Aufrechterhaltung kontaminationsfreier Bereiche, unterstützen Wissenschaftler und Ingenieure bei kleineren und mittelgroßen technischen Projekten, wirken bei Installations- und Implementierungsarbeiten mit und stellen sicher, dass Geräte und Verbrauchsmaterialien stets verfügbar und einsatzbereit sind. Ihre Arbeit trägt direkt zu hochpräzisen Laser-, Optik- und experimentellen Entwicklungsarbeiten bei, die für unsere Mission unerlässlich sind.


WAS SIE TUN WERDEN:

 - Reinraumbetrieb & -instandhaltung: Durchführung routinemäßiger und erweiterter Reinraumreinigungsarbeiten nach Protokoll, einschließlich Oberflächenreinigung, Bodenpflege, Austausch von Verbrauchsmaterialien und Umgebungsüberwachung, um die Einhaltung der Reinheitsklassifizierungen und internen Standards zu gewährleisten.

 - Technische Projektunterstützung: Unterstützung von Wissenschaftlern und Ingenieuren bei der Vorbereitung, Durchführung und dem Abschluss von kleinen und mittelgroßen Projekten – einschließlich Komponentenvorbereitung, grundlegenden mechanischen oder optischen Installationen, Kennzeichnung, Dokumentation und sicherer Materialhandhabung.

 - Beschaffung und Bestandsverwaltung: Bestellung, Nachverfolgung und Bereitstellung von Werkzeugen, Verbrauchsmaterialien, Laborbedarf und Reinraumausrüstung. Entwicklung und Pflege eines organisierten Systems zur Sicherstellung der Verfügbarkeit bei gleichzeitiger Minimierung von Verzögerungen und Engpässen.

 - Koordination von Werkzeugen und Ausrüstung: Unterstützung bei der Installation, Kalibrierung und grundlegenden Wartung von Reinraumausrüstung und Laborgeräten. Bei Bedarf Zusammenarbeit mit externen Anbietern für Service, Reparaturen, Inspektionen und Lieferungen.

 - Kleinere Bau- und Installationsarbeiten: Durchführung praktischer Arbeiten, die über die üblichen Aufgaben eines Reinraumtechnikers hinausgehen – einschließlich Unterstützung beim Aufbau der Infrastruktur, Installation von Gestellen, Kabelverlegung, Fertigung leichter mechanischer Bauteile, Montage von Vorrichtungen und Implementierung neuer Arbeitsabläufe oder Stationen.• Prozess- und Sicherheitskonformität: Gewährleisten Sie die strikte Einhaltung von Sicherheitsverfahren, Kontaminationskontrollprotokollen, PSA- Vorschriften und Materialflussregeln. Helfen Sie mit, Probleme proaktiv zu erkennen und Korrekturmaßnahmen zu unterstützen.

 - Dokumentation & Kontinuierliche Verbesserung: Führen genauer Protokolle, Checklisten und Inventarlisten.

 - Aufzeichnungen. Tragen Sie zur Verbesserung von Abläufen, zur Steigerung der betrieblichen Effizienz und zur Verbesserung der Nutzbarkeit und Zuverlässigkeit der Reinraumumgebung bei.


WER SIE SIND:

 - Reinraumerfahrung: Sie sind vertraut mit Hygiene- und Reinigungsprotokollen, Einschleuseprozessen sowie der Bedeutung von Präzision und Beständigkeit in technischer Umgebung. Erfahrung in Reinräumen oder anderen Laboren mit Kontaminationskontrolle ist von Vorteil.

 - Praktische technische Fähigkeiten: Vertrautheit mit Werkzeugen, Vorrichtungen, mechanischer Montage und Optikinstallationen. Sie können den technischen Anweisungen folgen und Laboraufbauten, Geräteumzüge oder Umbaumaßnahmen unterstützen.

 - Organisiert und detailorientiert: Ausgeprägte Fähigkeit, Ordnung in Bezug auf Lagerbestände, Material, Protokolle und Checkli

source_jobs.title: Diode Laser Engineer (f/m/d)
source_jobs.description:
Focused Energy is a fusion energy startup with the goal of generating unlimited clean energy to responsibly meet future global demand. With the use and commercialization of laser-induced inertial fusion, a clean and highly efficient power generation technology is coming within reach, suitable to ensure a secure and scalable future energy supply.

We are seeking a skilled and driven Diode Laser Engineer to help design, qualify, and scale the high-power diode laser technologies that will power our next-generation fusion laser systems. In this role, you will work at the intersection of physics, engineering, and industrialization — partnering closely with suppliers, research institutions, and internal system teams to advance the performance, reliability, and manufacturability of laser diode technologies.


WHAT YOU’LL DO:

 - Diode Technology Development: Design and evaluate high-power laser diode emitters, bars, and stacks to meet demanding performance, efficiency, lifetime, and cost targets for fusion laser applications.

 - Testing & Characterization: Develop and execute comprehensive electro-optical, thermal, and reliability test protocols (P-I-V, spectral behavior, beam quality, thermal resistance, lifetime, etc.) under relevant operating conditions including pulsed, CW, and high-duty-cycle regimes.

 - Automated Test Infrastructure: Design, commission, and operate automated test benches for high-volume characterization and accelerated life testing of commercial and custom laser diode devices.

 - Reliability & Lifetime Modeling: Lead accelerated life testing (ALT / HALT), analyze degradation mechanisms and failure modes, and build predictive lifetime and reliability models to inform system design and operational limits.

 - Supplier & Partner Collaboration: Work closely with industry partners, suppliers, national labs, and academic groups to improve diode performance, packaging, thermal management, and manufacturing readiness.

 - System Integration: Collaborate with DPSSL system and beamline teams to ensure diode technologies integrate effectively into laser modules and support overall system performance and scalability.

 - Data Analysis & Reporting: Analyze large experimental datasets, generate rigorous technical documentation, and communicate findings and recommendations clearly to internal teams and external partners.


WHO YOU ARE:

 - PhD (or equivalent experience) in optical, electrical, mechanical engineering, physics, or a related field, with deep experience in semiconductor laser physics, epitaxy, packaging, and/or device characterization.

 - Proven experience designing, building, and operating laser diode test and characterization setups, including optical, thermal, and electrical diagnostics.

 - Strong understanding of thermal management, reliability physics, degradation mechanisms, and lifetime testing methodologies for high-power semiconductor devices.

 - Comfortable working with large experimental datasets, applying statistical and modeling tools to extract insights, predict performance, and guide engineering decisions.

 - Able to work effectively across internal system teams and with external suppliers, academic collaborators, and industrial partners in complex, multi-stakeholder environments.

 - You naturally think about how component-level decisions affect system-level performance, cost, scalability, and long-term operational viability.

 - Preferred: Experience working with diode-pumped solid-state lasers (DPSSL), high-average-power laser systems, or adjacent fields such as industrial lasers, photonics manufacturing, or fusion/energy systems.

 - Preferred: Experience with thermal, optical, or reliability modeling tools to complement experimental work and support predictive design and scaling.


WHAT WE OFFER:

 - Innovative Technology & Mission: Be part of a cutting-edge company working on groundbreaking research in clean energy and scientific discovery.

source_jobs.title: People Enablement Analytics Partner (f/m/d)
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT FOCUSED ENERGY

Focused Energy is building the future of clean energy through laser-driven inertial fusion. Backed by our $240M Series A — the largest fully secured Series A in the global fusion industry — we are scaling rapidly across multiple geographies.

 

Based in Germany and the US, we have brought together the top scientific and engineering minds using proven technologies to unlock fusion power at commercial scale. Focused Energy is working to realize game-changing scientific innovation that promises to deliver a clean, sustainable and abundant source of power globally.




YOUR ROLE

This role sits at the intersection of Talent Acquisition, workforce planning, and People analytics. You will report directly to the VP of People and serve three connected mandates.

First, you will be the operational backbone of the Talent Acquisition function: owning systems, processes, and projects that make recruiting structured and scalable. Second, you will own and evolve our workforce planning process, connecting hiring demand, organizational growth, and capacity into a coherent forward view. Third, you will build and own the analytics and reporting layer for the broader People team, turning data from across the employee lifecycle into the transparency leadership needs to make good decisions.

This is a builder role. You will bring structure to a fast-moving environment, create reporting that people actually use, and help connect the dots between hiring, workforce planning, and broader People strategy.

You will work closely with TA leadership, recruiters, hiring managers, and the People team.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

TA & Process Design You coordinate and drive TA projects end to end. You translate recruiting needs into documented, scalable workflows. You spot gaps and inefficiencies before they become problems. You build the playbooks, templates, and guidelines that make hiring consistent across teams and locations, and you track execution so nothing quietly dies on the vine.

Recruiting Systems & Data Integrity You are the go-to person for our TA systems, especially Ashby and our workforce planning tools. You ensure configurations, data structures, and workflows actually reflect how we recruit. You partner with stakeholders to maintain data quality and build documentation so the tools get used properly, not just installed.

Workforce Planning You own the workforce planning process and tools. You connect headcount demand from the business with hiring capacity, budget, and organizational priorities. You maintain and improve our workforce planning models so that leadership has a reliable forward view of where the organization is heading, where the gaps are, and what it takes to close them. You ensure that workforce planning is not a one-time exercise but a living process that informs recruiting priorities and People strategy on an ongoing basis.

People Analytics & Reporting You build and maintain dashboards and reports across the People function: hiring pipeline and progress, time to hire, conversion rates, recruiter capacity, headcount actuals versus plan, attrition, onboarding effectiveness, and other metrics as the function matures. You turn data into insight and insight into recommendations. You establish a reporting rhythm that gives leadership and the People team the transparency to act, not just observe.

AI & Automation You identify where AI and automation can make People and TA more efficient. You build and test workflows for reporting, documentation, process support, and operational follow-up. You evaluate new ideas pragmatically and help the team adopt what works.




WHO YOU ARE

 - Experience in TA , Recruiting , People , People Analytics, workforce planning, HR systems, or a comparable function.

 - Strong project management instincts and the ability to bring order to complex, evolving work.

 - Confidence working with data, dashboards, and operational metrics across the employee lifecycle.

 - Strong Excel or Google

Founded by two world-renowned scientists and a seasoned entrepreneur behind several successful startups, we are commercializing laser-induced inertial fusion—a clean and highly efficient power generation technology poised to transform the energy landscape.



Role Overview

The Environment Health & Safety (EH&S) Manager establishes and leads the company’s Environment Health & Safety framework in Germany, enabling safe, compliant, and scalable operations across high-hazard technical environments and office settings. This role ensures compliance with German occupational safety requirements while partnering closely with US safety leadership and global operational teams to align principles, reporting, and cross-site learning. source_jobs.title: Vacuum Engineer (f/m/d)
source_jobs.description:
COMPANY OVERVIEW

Focused Energy is the leading laser-driven fusion company, pursuing the most promising path to clean, limitless fusion power. With locations in Darmstadt, Germany, and the San Francisco Bay Area, we bring together top scientific and engineering minds to unlock commercially viable fusion energy using proven technologies. We are driving the next chapter of economic growth and scientific innovation through the widespread adoption of clean, abundant fusion energy.




ROLE OVERVIEW

We are seeking a Vacuum Engineer with hands-on experience in both small-scale experimental systems and large-scale vacuum infrastructure. In this role, you will be responsible for the design, specification, integration, and operation of vacuum systems that support our laser, diagnostic, and reactor development platforms.

As part of a multidisciplinary team, you will work at the intersection of mechanical engineering, controls, and facility design. This position requires both practical engineering judgment and comprehensive system-level thinking.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES


SYSTEM DESIGN & ENGINEERING

 - Design and specify vacuum systems for:
   
   - Small experimental setups (e.g., optical benches, diagnostic chambers)
   
   - Medium-scale systems (e.g., beamlines, target chambers)
   
   - Large-scale infrastructure (e.g., facility vacuum systems, reactor-relevant environments)

 - Define vacuum system requirements (pressure ranges, pump-down times, cleanliness, leak rates)

 - Perform calculations for conductance, outgassing, pump sizing, and transient pump-down behavior




EQUIPMENT SPECIFICATION & INTEGRATION

 - Select and specify pumps (turbo, cryo, scroll, roots, backing systems), valves, gauges, seals, and feedthroughs

 - Choose vacuum-compatible materials and surface treatments

 - Interface with vendors and evaluate technical proposals

 - Support procurement of long-lead vacuum equipment




SYSTEM INTEGRATION

 - Integrate vacuum systems into laser systems, beam transport, target injection and tracking systems, diagnostic equipment, and facility infrastructure

 - Collaborate with controls engineers on pump sequencing, interlocks and safety systems, monitoring, and automation




BUILD, TEST & COMMISSIONING

 - Support assembly, installation, and commissioning of vacuum systems

 - Develop and implement leak testing procedures (e.g., helium leak detection)

 - Conduct pump-down testing and performance validation

 - Troubleshoot system issues such as leaks, contamination, and instability




OPERATIONS & CONTINUOUS IMPROVEMENT

 - Develop operating procedures and maintenance plans

 - Enhance system reliability, uptime, and overall performance

 - Support scaling efforts from prototype to facility-level deployment

CORE EXPERIENCE

 - Degree in Mechanical Engineering, Physics, or a related field

 - 3–10+ years of experience working with vacuum systems

 - Proven experience in both laboratory/experimental and large/industrial vacuum systems




TECHNICAL EXPERTISE

 - Strong understanding of vacuum physics (flow regimes, outgassing, conductance)

 - Expertise in pump technologies, sealing methods (CF, KF, ISO, elastomer vs metal seals), and vacuum system design and calculations

 - Hands-on experience with leak detection, troubleshooting, and working with clean or ultra-clean systems (preferred)

 - Familiarity with chemical and/or particulate vacuum cleanliness testing (e.g., RGA, NVR, particle measurement) is a plus




PRACTICAL SKILLS

 - Experience building and commissioning vacuum systems

 - Ability to read and produce engineering drawings (Inventor or similar CAD tools)

 - Experience collaborating with suppliers and managing technical specifications

Focused Energy is an international deep-tech company based in Germany and the US, working to commercialize laser-driven nuclear fusion. We are seeking a Supply Chain Engineer to lead the sourcing, development, and delivery of complex engineering systems and components across our programs.

This is not a traditional procurement role. You will operate at the intersection of engineering, manufacturing, and supplier development, ensuring that critical components—from precision optics and vacuum systems to mechanical assemblies and control hardware—are technically viable, manufacturable, and delivered on time.

With the use and commercialization of laser-induced inertial fusion, a clean and highly efficient power generation technology is coming within reach, suitable to ensure a secure and scalable future energy supply.

We are seeking an Optical Engineer for our Digital Twin efforts to develop, validate, and apply advanced optical and multiphysics simulation tools that form the backbone of our laser Digital Twin. As part of our Laser team, you will integrate optical and multiphysics models into a coherent, predictive framework that supports system design, optimization, and experimental validation of our laser point designs. Your work will directly reduce design risk, accelerate development cycles, and enable system-level optimization of the high-energy laser systems powering our fusion program.

 - Validate simulation models against experimental data and perform sensitivity and uncertainty analysis

 - Experience in model validation, uncertainty quantification, and data-driven refinement

 - Ability to communicate complex simulation results clearly to non-specialists

 - Strong collaboration skills across interdisciplinary teams

 - Structured and analytical problem-solving approach

 - Ability to work independently while aligning with broader system goals

PREFERRED

 - Familiarity with MDO workflows (UQ, DoE, ML training) and metamodelling / surrogate techniques (ROM, Digital Twin)

 - Interest in fusion energy, high-energy-density physics, or advanced laser technologies

 - Hands-on R&D testing experience with laser systems or optomechanical assemblies

 - Proactive approach to improving simulation workflows and methodologies

source_jobs.title: Electrical Technician (f/m/d)

ABOUT FOCUSED ENERGY

Focused Energy is an international deep-tech company based in Germany and the US, working to commercialize laser-driven nuclear fusion. Our mission is to provide clean, safe, and virtually unlimited energy to the world. We are rapidly scaling and looking for talented individuals who thrive in building clarity and structure within fast-growing environments.

Join us as an Electrical Technician and help to provide the clean energy of the future!

In this role, you will play a key role in building, wiring, maintaining, and testing critical infrastructure and custom equipment used across our experimental and production environments to ensure high reliability and safety of electrical components and systems. Focused Energy is a laser-driven fusion company building technologies that can unlock clean, abundant energy. Alongside our long-term mission, we also develop near-term, high-impact applications based on compact laser-driven radiation sources that combine MeV X-rays and fast neutrons in a single system for non-destructive testing and advanced inspection.




ROLE OVERVIEW

We are looking for a Project Manager to lead the execution of the worlds first laser-driven radiation source prototype, bringing together multiple subsystem owners and engineering teams to deliver a research-operable prototype. This role is ideal for someone who enjoys turning complex technical development into a structured plan, enabling teams to deliver on time, and building an operational hardware system.

You will work together with the main system owners within the project make sure the right work happens at the right time, with clear interfaces, controlled changes, and strong transparency.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Own the integrated project plan: maintain the master schedule across subsystems, dependencies, and critical path; drive recovery plans when milestones slip.

 - Lead project governance and cadence: run core meetings, maintain decisions and actions, and ensure consistent status reporting that highlights progress, blockers, and next steps.

 - Align work to the technical roadmap: ensure subsystem plans and milestones stay consistent with the program direction; surface deviations early and drive resolution.

 - Process improvement: continuously improve delivery flow and documentation efficiency with the Project Management Office to reduce redundancy and increase execution speed, without compromising safety or quality.

 - Communication: clear, concise communicator who can lead meetings, write crisp status updates, and drive action closure with senior stakeholders.

 - Operational rigor: able to support readiness/commissioning planning and ensure documentation and evidence are produced at a level that enables repeatable operation.

source_jobs.description:
About Focused Energy

At Focused Energy, we are pioneering SourceLight—a breakthrough initiative in compact, laser-driven radiation sources (LDRS) with cutting-edge, high-power laser technology. Our dual-mode LDRS platform enables real-time, non-destructive inspection of a wide range of materials, including complex and shielded items, by combining MeV X-rays and fast neutrons in a single, modular system. This technology addresses critical challenges across nuclear waste analysis, contraband detection, infrastructure inspection, and medical isotope production. With its unique combination of high resolution, material specificity, and deployment flexibility, LDRS creates opportunities beyond the limits of conventional systems. Our cross-disciplinary team brings together experts in laser and plasma physics, engineering, and AI. We are building our first demonstrator facility in Biblis, Germany, and moving rapidly toward market readiness and commercial deployment. Join us at the ground floor of this deep-tech venture and help shape the future of radiation source technology.

Role Scope & Context


As a Safety Engineer, you will ensure that safety is systematically integrated into the design, construction, commissioning, and operation of LDRS facilities and systems. The role supports the identification, assessment, and mitigation of hazards across complex, high‑energy and high‑risk technologies, enabling safe and compliant operations while supporting rapid technical development. Its deliverables will shape the installation requirements and procedures for future LDRS products at customer sites.

 - Drive hazard identification and risk assessments for LDRS facility and selected subsystems.

source_jobs.description:
Company Overview

 

Focused Energy is the leading laser-driven fusion company, dedicated to creating clean, limitless fusion power. We unite top scientific and engineering talent to unlock commercially viable fusion energy using proven technologies. Our goal is to drive economic growth and scientific innovation through the adoption of clean, abundant fusion energy worldwide.

 

Your Role

 

As a Cryogenics Engineer (f/m/d), you will play a pivotal role in designing, modeling, and optimizing advanced cryogenic thermal and fluid systems for commercial-scale fusion energy solutions. You will collaborate with interdisciplinary teams to develop, simulate, and validate experimental designs, helping define the roadmap toward efficient and scalable clean energy.

 

What You’ll Do

 

 - Own the end-to-end simulation and analysis function for cryogenic thermal and fluid systems—from problem definition through model development, validation, and continuous improvement.

 - Lead analytical approaches for new and undefined challenges, applying first-principles research to create pioneering solutions.

 - Drive model validation through close collaboration with experimental engineers, comparing simulation outputs to test data, and closing performance gaps.

 - Ensure that the Cryo team’s hardware decisions are consistently supported by robust quantitative analysis.

 - Develop integrated thermal and fluid analysis packages for commercial-scale system development, conducting optimization and sensitivity studies as needed.

 - Continuously refine analysis models as new experimental, materials, and system data become available.

 - Partner closely with hardware engineers, experimentalists, and scientists to define system requirements and deliver actionable analyses for design.

 - Provide the analytical foundation for the Cryo team’s technical roadmap, guiding what gets built, tested, and scaled.

 

Who You Are

 

 - 3–5 years’ experience (or equivalent PhD research) in integrated thermal and fluid analysis involving cryogenic systems (experience with hydrogen is preferred).

 - Strong foundation in heat transfer, thermodynamics, and fluid mechanics.

 - Proficient in thermal analysis software (e.g., Thermal Desktop, COMSOL) and able to anchor analyses to real hardware.

 - Familiarity with hydrogen systems, vacuum systems, cryogenic technologies, and phase change in solids, liquids, and gases.

Focused Energy is looking for a Software Engineer II to join our team and build AI-assisted design workflows that help our mechanical and physical engineering teams turn complex requirements into design solutions and CAD drawings faster. Today, the mechanical engineering team relies on manual CAD creation for complex parts, a process that slows down design exploration and delays experimental iteration as we scale toward commercial fusion power.

In this role, you will design, build, and deploy internal software tools and automated workflows that generate CAD and part drawings from complex physical and mechanical requirements. ver the next 6–18 months, you'll help establish the standards, runbooks, and training that allow mechanical engineers to safely and effectively use AI-generated CAD outputs, directly accelerating our design exploration speed as we move toward commercial-scale fusion.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Design and build scalable, maintainable internal software tools and workflows that automate the generation of CAD and part drawings from complex requirements.

 - Translate physical and mechanical requirements into clear software designs, parametric CAD scripts, and automated design workflows.

 - Teach mechanical and hardware engineers how to intake requirements and iterate on design solutions using AI-assisted CAD workflows.

 - Collaborate with mechanical engineering, software, and systems engineering teams to ensure generated CAD drawings meet strict physical and operational constraints.

 - Establish standards and runbooks to help mechanical engineers review and validate AI-generated CAD outputs before manufacturing.

 - Systematically debug and troubleshoot complex workflows across system boundaries, including CAD software integrations and data pipelines.




WHO YOU ARE

 - Experience as an independent software engineer who can design, build, and deploy maintainable services, tools, or software components.

 - Experience writing scripts or software that interacts with CAD systems, mechanical design tools, or parametric design APIs.

 - Hands-on experience implementing AI-assisted workflows, automation scripts, or agentic frameworks.

 - Write clear technical runbooks, usage guides, and training material for technical users.

 - Strong programming skills in languages such as Python or C++ and familiarity with CAD software APIs (such as SolidWorks, Onshape, or AutoCAD).

 - Solid understanding of parametric CAD, 3D modeling, part drawings, and mechanical requirements translation.

 - Communicate clearly and collaborate effectively with mechanical engineers, software teams, and scientific partners.

 - Comfortable in high-ambiguity environments where priorities and hardware requirements can evolve.

 - Strong respect for safety and engineering review processes when using AI-generated designs.

 - Preferred: experience in mechanical engineering, manufacturing, physical modeling, aerospace, target fabrication, or nuclear fusion design.

 - Preferred: experience building developer productivity tools, CI/CD systems, or automated testing infrastructure for scientific or design workflows.

 - Preferred: experience with advanced CAD scripting, geometric processing, or automated design generation APIs.
